item.title: 如何在您的 Nodejs 代码中实现向后兼容性？ | How to achieve backward compatibility in your Nodejs code?
item.description:
奥马尔·杜莱米

发表于为了实现向后兼容，有时我们必须为不同的𝗡𝗼𝗱𝗲𝗷𝘀版本编写单独的代码。

这是由于 API 被弃用而其他 API 成为其官方替代品的结果。

幸运的是，有一些方法可以处理这个问题。 今天我就讲一个。

有了这条信息，我们可以根据我们获得的版本有条件地提供/运行代码。

一个名为𝘀𝗲𝗺𝘃𝗲𝗿 的流行库使版本号比较非常简单，因此在这里使用它。

如果你密切关注我的帖子，你应该注意到我这次没有写𝗻𝗼𝗱𝗲：𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀； 因为它是一个新概念，在低于 8 的版本中不可用。

你今天学到新东西了吗？

喜欢并分享这篇文章，并关注我以获取更多信息！

一旦暂停，omardulaimi 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，omardulaimi 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，omardulaimi 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 omardulaimi 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 omardulaimi 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 omardulaimi 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Omar Dulaimi

Posted on Aug 5

To achieve backward compatibility, sometimes we have to write separate code for different 𝗡𝗼𝗱𝗲𝗷𝘀 versions.

This happens as a result of APIs getting deprecated and others emerging as their official replacements.

Fortunately, there are ways to handle this issue. Today I'll be talking about one.

The 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀 module provides you with the runtime version of your 𝗡𝗼𝗱𝗲𝗷𝘀 that is currently executing your script. With this piece of info, we could conditionally serve/run code based on the version we get.

A popular library called 𝘀𝗲𝗺𝘃𝗲𝗿 makes version number comparisons super simple, hence why it's used here.

If you pay close attention to my posts, you should notice that I didn't write 𝗻𝗼𝗱𝗲:𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀 this time; since it's a new concept that wasn't available in versions below 8.

Did you learn something new today?

Like and share this post, and follow me for more!

Once suspended, omardulaimi will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, omardulaimi will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by omardulaimi will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If omardulaimi is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag omardulaimi:

omardulaimi consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging omardulaimi will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: omardulaimi

item.title: 在 Nodejs 中构建一个字符串电话 | Build a String Telephone in Nodejs
item.description:
奥马尔·杜莱米

发表于我认为是时候提供一个实用有趣的提示了！

您是否知道可以使用 𝗽𝗶𝗽𝗲 方法将标准输入流通过管道传输到标准输出流？

这让你得到一个镜像效果； 您输入的任何内容都会在终端上回显给您。

自己试试吧！

希望这能让我在以后的帖子中谈论流，因为它们在 𝗡𝗼𝗱𝗲𝗷𝘀 社区中被低估了。

你今天学到新东西了吗？

喜欢并分享这篇文章，并关注我以获取更多信息！

穆罕默德·纳兹穆尔·侯赛因 - 一旦暂停，omardulaimi 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，omardulaimi 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，omardulaimi 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 omardulaimi 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 omardulaimi 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 omardulaimi 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Omar Dulaimi

Posted on Aug 5

I think it's time for a practical fun tip!

Did you know you could pipe the standard input stream to the standard output stream using the 𝗽𝗶𝗽𝗲 method?

This let's you get a mirroring effect; where whatever you type will be echoed back to you on the terminal.

Give it a try yourself!

Hopefully this will get me to talk about streams in my future posts, as they are so underrated in the 𝗡𝗼𝗱𝗲𝗷𝘀 community.

Did you learn something new today?

Like and share this post, and follow me for more!

Mohammad Nazmul Hossain - Aug 5

Once unsuspended, omardulaimi will be able to comment and publish posts again.

If omardulaimi is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 通过解组 Go 结构将微服务 JSON 响应中继到客户端 | Relaying a Microservice JSON Response to the Client by Unmarshalling Go Structs
item.description:
在这篇文章中，我们将通过添加诸如名称、日期或唯一标识符之类的元数据并将其中继到用户客户端来转换来自微服务的 JSON 响应。 这是我在 iOS 上为研究人员构建 AI SaaS 应用程序的一部分，作为示例，我们将假装将数据发送到动物园动物的 AI 对象标识符。

过去一周，我一直致力于将图像分析微服务连接到用户前端。 在这篇文章中，我将讨论如何转换 JSON 响应并添加元数据，如名称、日期或唯一标识符。 您还可以转换数据，因此我们可以将 X-Min 中继为坐标和width而不是x_max ，而不是转发x_min和x_max坐标。 为了在客户端区分对象是由算法还是人类检测到，我们添加了一个新的字段method 。 这也可能是一个更复杂的对象来存储 AI 算法的版本。 中继的响应应如下所示

{ "name" : "Zoo Image Analysis" , "xid" : "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" "predictions" : [ { "confidence" : 0.69146144 , "label" : "Lion" , "x" : 682 , "y" : 109 , "width" : 77 , // TODO calculate actual width "height" : 25 , "method" : "AI" }, { "confidence" : 0.7 , "label" : "Penguin" , "x" : 782 , "y" : 209 , "width" : - 13 , "height" : - 65 , "method" : "AI" }] }

为此，我们将创建一个ResponseAdapter结构来模仿 AI 微服务的响应结构以及PredictionAdapter 。 然后我们创建一个Response和一个Prediction结构，其中包含我们想要传递给客户端的字段和方法。

  1. 使用空接口打印未知 JSON 数据
  2. 将 JSON 数据转换为可用的结构以访问数据字段
  3. 重写为可测试的函数
  4. 实现自定义 UnmarshalJSON
  5. 结论

从 API 检索一些 JSON 数据时，我们不能像data.name那样调用这些属性。 将这些数据转换为结构需要一些解组、映射和接口。 因此，让我们通过一些 JSON 处理示例逐步实现我们的目标，即使来自 API 调用的数据可用。

您可以使用 VS Code 中的本地工具和 go-tools 执行这些步骤，或者直接使用Go Playground ，无需任何安装。

使用空接口打印未知 JSON 数据

为了获得一些示例数据，我们将一些来自 AI 微服务的 JSON 数据作为多行字符串读取到字节数组中。

 var data = [] byte ( `{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }` )

接下来我们创建一个临时的空接口

var temp map [ string ] interface {}

然后将数据解组到这个变量 temp 中。 这需要导入„encoding/json“ 。

 json . Unmarshal ( data , & temp )

捕获任何解组错误

if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) }

然后我们可以打印出temp。

结果应该是

map [ predictions : [ map [ confidence : 0.69146144 label : Lion x_max : 186 x_min : 109 y_max : 707 y_min : 682 ] map [ confidence : 0.7 label : Penguin x_max : 196 x_min : 209 y_max : 717 y_min : 782 ]] success : true ]

伟大的。 如果我们只想访问标题

fmt . Println ( temp . predictions [ 0 ])

我们得到一个错误。

 ./ prog . go : 40 : 19 : temp . predictions undefined ( type map [ string ] interface {} has no field or method predictions )

好的，接下来让我们关注这个。 本节的清单。

 package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" ) func main () { var data = [] byte ( `{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }` ) var temp map [ string ] interface {} //var prediction PredictionsAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & temp ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } //fmt.Println(temp.predictions[0]) }

访问数据字段

假设我们事先知道预期的数据结构，让我们创建一个结构。

 type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions [] PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"` }

向上滚动到第一个 JSON 响应以查看其来源。 这反映了我们从 AI 微服务获得的响应。 要导出，名称必须以大写字母开头。 我们用两个函数Width()和Height()扩展PredictionsAdapter ，因为我们稍后需要这些信息来为我们的客户响应Predictions 。

 func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Width () int { return p . XMax - p . XMin } func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Height () int { return p . YMax - p . YMin }

我们没有将其解组为空接口，而是使用ResponseAdapter类型的变量。

 var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter json . Unmarshall ( data , & responseAdapter ) fmt . Println ( responseAdapter . Predictions [ 0 ])

这就是我们如何从 go as structs 访问 JSON 数据的方式:)。

 { 0.69146144 Lion 682 109 707 186 }

完整列表

package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" ) type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions [] PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"` } type PredictionsAdapter struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` YMin int `json:"y_min"` XMin int `json:"x_min"` YMax int `json:"y_max"` XMax int `json:"x_max"` } func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Width () int { return p . XMax - p . XMin } func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Height () int { return p . YMax - p . YMin } func main () { var data = [] byte ( `{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }` ) //var temp map[string]interface{} var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & responseAdapter ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } fmt . Println ( responseAdapter . Predictions [ 0 ]) }

测试

我发现写一些有用的东西并首先看到一些结果总是很有动力。 为了适应良好的实践，让我们现在将所有内容重写为可测试的函数并摆脱main() 。 如果你使用 Go Playground，它会在没有main()的情况下自动运行测试函数。

首先导入"testing"和"github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp" 。 它是一个want got与函数的实际结果进行比较的库。 它检查两者是否与if !cmp.Equal(want, got)相等，否则返回带有差异的错误。

我们从功能上下文中插入data声明，以便可以从每个测试功能访问它。

我们将在main中发生的大部分内容重命名为ParseImageAnalysisResponse ，它将接收[]byte类型的 JSON 内容并返回一个ResponseAdapter和一个error 。

测试函数具有相同的名称，带有前缀TestXxx和对*testing.T的引用，它可以使用go test在 go 中进行自动化测试。 然后第一行使用t.Parallel()启用并行测试执行。

然后我们声明我们的预期结果。 这次我们不使用 JSON 格式，因为我们期望 data 作为 go 结构。 这就是为什么我们在打开花括号之前声明类型的原因want := ResponseAdapter{... .}

 want := ResponseAdapter { Success : true , Predictions : [] PredictionsAdapter { { Confidence : 0.69146144 , Label : "Lion" , YMin : 682 , XMin : 109 , YMax : 707 , XMax : 186 , }, { Confidence : 0.7 , Label : "Penguin" , YMin : 782 , XMin : 209 , YMax : 717 , XMax : 196 , }, }, }

然后我们必须用我们的data调用函数并捕获可能的错误。 最后我们比较期望和结果。

 if ! cmp . Equal ( want , got ) { t . Error ( cmp . Diff ( want , got )) }

如果一切正确，我们可以使用 Run on Go Playground 或在命令行中进行go test ，测试应该通过或返回错误。

 === RUN TestParseImageAnalysisResponse === PAUSE TestParseImageAnalysisResponse === CONT TestParseImageAnalysisResponse { 0.69146144 Lion 682 109 707 186 } --- PASS : TestParseImageAnalysisResponse ( 0.00 s ) PASS

完整列表

package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" "github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp" "testing" ) type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions [] PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"` } type PredictionsAdapter struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` YMin int `json:"y_min"` XMin int `json:"x_min"` YMax int `json:"y_max"` XMax int `json:"x_max"` } var data = [] byte ( `{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }` ) func ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( data [] byte ) ( ResponseAdapter , error ) { var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & responseAdapter ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } fmt . Println ( responseAdapter . Predictions [ 0 ]) return responseAdapter , nil } func TestParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( t * testing . T ) { t . Parallel () want := ResponseAdapter { Success : true , Predictions : [] PredictionsAdapter { { Confidence : 0.69146144 , Label : "Lion" , YMin : 682 , XMin : 109 , YMax : 707 , XMax : 186 , }, { Confidence : 0.7 , Label : "Penguin" , YMin : 782 , XMin : 209 , YMax : 717 , XMax : 196 , }, }, } got , err := ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( data ) if err != nil { t . Fatal ( err ) } if ! cmp . Equal ( want , got ) { t . Error ( cmp . Diff ( want , got )) } }

现在我们有了可以实际使用的格式的数据。 我们甚至可以将其转换为 JSON 并将其传递给我们的用户客户端。 但有时实现逻辑需要我们添加或删除一些字段，并以不同的格式提供数据。 如果这是最有意义的，我们应该这样做以维护一个设计良好的数据工作流，即使它需要一些额外的工作。

在下一节中，我们将以我们实际需要的方式定义Response和Predictions类型，并使用自定义UnmarshallJSON从我们的 Adapter 类型转换它们。 首先，我们将添加一些自定义元数据。 但是，在[]PredictionsAdapter中添加或更改字段需要遍历数组中的每个元素。

实现自定义 UnmarshallJSON

让我们为接下来要实现的ParseImageAnalysisResponse创建测试（忽略任何错误）。 我们使用数据将其传递给ParseImageAnalysisResponse ，然后查看是否满足我们的期望。

 func TestParseImageAnalysisResponse ( t * testing . T ) { t . Parallel () want := Response { Name : "Test Image" , Xid : "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" , Predictions : [] Prediction { { Confidence : 0.69146144 , Label : "Lion" , X : 109 , Y : 682 , Width : 77 , Height : 25 , Method : "AI" , }, { Confidence : 0.7 , Label : "Penguin" , X : 209 , Y : 782 , Width : - 13 , Height : - 65 , Method : "AI" , }, }, } got , err := ParseImageAnalysisResponse ( data ) if err != nil { t . Fatal ( err ) } if ! cmp . Equal ( want , got ) { t . Error ( cmp . Diff ( want , got )) } }

接下来，我们定义与我们想要中继到客户端的 JSON API 响应相匹配的类型，可以是 Web 应用程序或 Android 或 iOS 的移动应用程序。

 type Prediction struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` X int `json:"x"` Y int `json:"y"` Width int `json:"width"` Height int `json:"height"` Method string `json:"method"` // AI, Manual, Predicted(?) }

响应将包含许多这些预测，因为可以在图像中检测到许多对象。 但更进一步，它将包括元数据，如Name和Xid 。 稍后我们可能需要更改类型，但现在让我们使用string 。

 type Response struct { Name string `json:"name"` Xid string `json:"xid"` Predictions [] Prediction `json:"predictions"` }

现在我们将调整ParseImageAnalysisResponse函数。 它仍将使用[]byte类型的data作为参数，但它现在将返回我们的新Response ResponseAdapter 因此，我们使用Response类型的变量response并在解组时使用它。 然后我们检查错误并返回response 。

 func ParseImageAnalysisResponse ( data [] byte ) ( Response , error ) { var response Response // Use custom UnmarshalJSON method to obtain Response err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & response ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } return response , nil }

由于从ResponseAdapter到Response没有简单的一对一映射，我们需要做一些手动工作。 代替我们不必指定的默认UnmarshalJSON ，我们现在将使用它的自定义版本，明确说明我们如何从Response中的数据派生对客户端的ResponseAdapter 。
我们发送给ParseImageAnalysisResponse的data是我们想要解组的数据。 我们知道我们可以将它解组为一个ResponseAdapter ，所以这就是我们首先要做的，但不是解组到响应对象r的指针中，而是解组到一个临时变量tmp中。

 var tmp ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & tmp ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) }

此变量还具有 PredictionsAdapter 形式的PredictionsAdapter 。 我们现在将它们转换成一个新的 Predictions-Array。

我们通过循环每个元素PredictionsAdapter来做到这一点

for _ , element := range tmp . Predictions {

并创建一个临时对应的预测元素。
这个 predictions 元素还定义了任何添加的字段的数据，或者它也可以从PredictionsAdapter中省略字段。 然后我们将 Predictions 元素附加到在循环之前定义的 Predictions Array p中。 我在这里一步完成了变量创建和附加，并关闭了循环。

 p = append ( p , Prediction { Confidence : element . Confidence , Method : "AI" , Label : element . Label , XMin : element . XMin , XMax : element . XMax , YMin : element . YMin , YMax : element . YMax , }) }

然后我们设置Response字段。 这包括名称和 Xid 等元数据以及设置预测数组 p。

 r . Name = "Test Image" r . Xid = "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" r . Predictions = p

最后，由于 UnmarshalJSON 要求我们返回错误，我们也这样做。

现在让我们看看这个函数的完整外观。

 func ( r * Response ) UnmarshalJSON ( data [] byte ) error { var tmp ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & tmp ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } var p [] Prediction for _ , element := range tmp . Predictions { p = append ( p , Prediction { Confidence : element . Confidence , Method : "AI" , Label : element . Label , X : element . XMin , Y : element . YMin , Width : element . Width (), Height : element . Height (), }) } r . Name = "Test Image" r . Xid = "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" r . Predictions = p return err }

结论

伟大的。 我们现在可以拍摄一张带有 AI 微服务注释的图像，并通过使用更相关的 JSON 响应数据对其进行增强，从而使该分析对于将其转发给用户客户端很有用。 在网络方面，解释了如何将来自 HTTP 请求的 JSON 响应准确地发送到客户端。 我在上一篇文章中谈到了如何向 AI 微服务发送 POST 请求。

最后，让我们看看完整的清单，您也可以在Go Playground上找到它。 当然，测试也可以写入单独的文件。

感谢阅读，我希望你觉得这很有用。 这是我新博客上的前五篇文章之一，所以我感谢任何意见和建议。

 package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" "github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp" "testing" ) type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions [] PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"` } type PredictionsAdapter struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` YMin int `json:"y_min"` XMin int `json:"x_min"` YMax int `json:"y_max"` XMax int `json:"x_max"` } func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Width () int { return p . XMax - p . XMin } func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Height () int { return p . YMax - p . YMin } type Prediction struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` X int `json:"x"` Y int `json:"y"` Width int `json:"width"` Height int `json:"height"` Method string `json:"method"` // AI, Manual, Predicted(?) } type Response struct { Name string `json:"name"` Xid string `json:"xid"` Predictions [] Prediction `json:"predictions"` } func ( r * Response ) UnmarshalJSON ( data [] byte ) error { var tmp ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & tmp ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } var p [] Prediction for _ , element := range tmp . Predictions { // fmt.Println("element", element, "at index", index) p = append ( p , Prediction { Confidence : element . Confidence , Method : "AI" , Label : element . Label , X : element . XMin , Y : element . YMin , Width : element . Width (), Height : element . Height (), }) } r . Name = "Test Image" r . Xid = "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" r . Predictions = p return err } var data = [] byte ( `{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }` ) func ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( data [] byte ) ( ResponseAdapter , error ) { var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & responseAdapter ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } fmt . Println ( responseAdapter . Predictions [ 0 ]) return responseAdapter , nil } func ParseImageAnalysisResponse ( data [] byte ) ( Response , error ) { var response Response // Use custom UnmarshalJSON method to obtain Response err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & response ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } // fmt.Println("Response") // fmt.Println(response) return response , nil } func TestParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( t * testing . T ) { t . Parallel () want := ResponseAdapter { Success : true , Predictions : [] PredictionsAdapter { { Confidence : 0.69146144 , Label : "Lion" , YMin : 682 , XMin : 109 , YMax : 707 , XMax : 186 , }, { Confidence : 0.7 , Label : "Penguin" , YMin : 782 , XMin : 209 , YMax : 717 , XMax : 196 , }, }, } got , err := ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( data ) if err != nil { t . Fatal ( err ) } if ! cmp . Equal ( want , got ) { t . Error ( cmp . Diff ( want , got )) } } func TestParseImageAnalysisResponse ( t * testing . T ) { t . Parallel () want := Response { Name : "Test Image" , Xid : "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" , Predictions : [] Prediction { { Confidence : 0.69146144 , Label : "Lion" , X : 109 , Y : 682 , Width : 77 , Height : 25 , Method : "AI" , }, { Confidence : 0.7 , Label : "Penguin" , X : 209 , Y : 782 , Width : - 13 , Height : - 65 , Method : "AI" , }, }, } got , err := ParseImageAnalysisResponse ( data ) if err != nil { t . Fatal ( err ) } if ! cmp . Equal ( want , got ) { t . Error ( cmp . Diff ( want , got )) } }

链接

https://bitfieldconsulting.com/golang/map-string-interface https://bitfieldconsulting.com/golang/map-string-interface
https://jhall.io/posts/go-json-tricks-array-as-structs/ https://jhall.io/posts/go-json-tricks-array-as-structs/
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/42377989/unmarshal-json-array-of-arrays-in-go https://stackoverflow.com/questions/42377989/unmarshal-json-array-of-arrays-in-go



原文:

In this post we will transform a JSON response from a microservice by adding metadata like a name, date or a unique identifier and relaying it to the user client. This is part of my journey in building AI SaaS app on iOS for researchers, as an example we will pretend to send data to an AI object identifier for zoo animals.

The past week I have been working on connecting an image analysis microservice to the user frontend. We can send an image via post request and retrieve an array of x and y-coordinates as well as a label for the identified object and a confidence parameter.

{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Pinguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }]
}

In this post I will talk about transforming the JSON response and adding metadata like a name, date or a unique identifier. You could also convert the data, so instead of forwarding x_min and x_max coordinates, we could relay X-Min as coordinate and width instead of x_max. In order to distinguish on the client if an object was detected by an algorithm or a human we add a new field method. This could also be a more complicated object to store the version of the AI algorithm. The relayed response should look like this

{"name": "Zoo Image Analysis", "xid": "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "x": 682, "y": 109, "width": 77, // TODO calculate actual width "height": 25, "method": "AI" }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "x": 782, "y": 209, "width": -13, "height": -65, "method": "AI" }]
}

To do this we will create a ResponseAdapter struct that mimics the response structure of the AI microservice as well as a PredictionAdapter. Then we create a Response and a Prediction struct that contains the fields and methods we want to relay to our client.

  1. Print unknown JSON data with an empty interface
  2. Convert JSON data into a usable struct to access data fields
  3. Rewrite into testable functions
  4. Implementing custom UnmarshalJSON
  5. Conclusion

When retrieving some JSON data from an API, we cannot call those properties like with data.name. Converting this data into structs takes some unmarshalling, mapping and interfacing. So let’s play with some JSON handling examples to step by step arrive at our goal, which is making data from an API call usable.

You can follow these steps using your local tooling in VS Code and go-tools or just use Go Playground without any installation.

Printing unknown JSON data with an empty interface

To have some sample data, we read some JSON data from an AI microservice as a multiline string into a byte array.

var data = []byte(`{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }`)

Next we create a temporary empty interface

var temp map[string]interface{}

Then wir unmarshall the data into this variable temp. This requires an import of „encoding/json“.

json.Unmarshal(data, &temp)

Catch any unmarshalling errors

if err != nil { fmt.Println(err)
}

Then we can print out temp.

The result should be

map[predictions:[map[confidence:0.69146144 label:Lion x_max:186 x_min:109 y_max:707 y_min:682] map[confidence:0.7 label:Penguin x_max:196 x_min:209 y_max:717 y_min:782]] success:true]

Great. If we want to access just the title

fmt.Println(temp.predictions[0])

we get an error.

./prog.go:40:19: temp.predictions undefined (type map[string]interface{} has no field or method predictions)

Ok, let’s take care about this next. The listing for this section.

package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt"
) func main() { var data = []byte(`{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }`) var temp map[string]interface{} //var prediction PredictionsAdapter err := json.Unmarshal(data, &temp) if err != nil { fmt.Println(err) } //fmt.Println(temp.predictions[0])
}

Accessing data fields

Assuming we know the expected data structure in advance, lets create a struct.

type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions []PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"`
}

Scroll up to the first JSON response to see where this is coming from. This is mirroring the response we get from the AI microservice. To be exported, the names must start with a capital letter. []PredictionsAdapter means that we expect multiple objects or an array of type PredictionsAdapter.

type PredictionsAdapter struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` YMin int `json:"y_min"` XMin int `json:"x_min"` YMax int `json:"y_max"` XMax int `json:"x_max"`
}

We extend the PredictionsAdapter with two functions Width() and Height(), since we need this information later for our client response with Predictions.

func (p PredictionsAdapter) Width() int { return p.XMax - p.XMin
} func (p PredictionsAdapter) Height() int { return p.YMax - p.YMin
}

Instead of unmarshalling it into an empty interface, we use a variable of type ResponseAdapter.

var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter json.Unmarshall(data, &responseAdapter) fmt.Println(responseAdapter.Predictions[0])

The & implies that we are referring to the address in memory. And thats how we can access JSON data from go as structs :).

{0.69146144 Lion 682 109 707 186}

The full listing

package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt"
) type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions []PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"`
} type PredictionsAdapter struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` YMin int `json:"y_min"` XMin int `json:"x_min"` YMax int `json:"y_max"` XMax int `json:"x_max"`
} func (p PredictionsAdapter) Width() int { return p.XMax - p.XMin
} func (p PredictionsAdapter) Height() int { return p.YMax - p.YMin
} func main() { var data = []byte(`{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }`) //var temp map[string]interface{} var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter err := json.Unmarshal(data, &responseAdapter) if err != nil { fmt.Println(err) } fmt.Println(responseAdapter.Predictions[0])
}

Testing

I find it always motivating to write something that works and see some result first. To adapt good practices let’s now rewrite everything into a testable function and get rid of main(). If you use Go Playground, it will automatically run the test functions in absence of main().

First import "testing" and "github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp". It is a library to compare expectations want with actual result got of a function. It checks if both are equal with if !cmp.Equal(want, got) and returns an error with the differences otherwise.

The declaration of datawe plug out of the functional context such that it is accessible from each testing function.

Most of what has been happening in mainwe will rename to ParseImageAnalysisResponse which will receive the JSON content of type []byte and return a ResponseAdapter and an error.

The test function has the same name with prefix TestXxx and a reference to *testing.T which enables automated testing in go with go test. The first line then enables parallel test execution with t.Parallel().

Then we declare our expected result. This time we don’t use the JSON format, since we expect data as a go struct. Thats why we state the type before opening curly parenthesises want := ResponseAdapter{... .}

want := ResponseAdapter{ Success: true, Predictions: []PredictionsAdapter{ { Confidence: 0.69146144, Label: "Lion", YMin: 682, XMin: 109, YMax: 707, XMax: 186, }, { Confidence: 0.7, Label: "Penguin", YMin: 782, XMin: 209, YMax: 717, XMax: 196, }, },
}

Then we have to call the function with our data and catch possible errors. got will be of type ResponseAdapter, since this is what ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter(…) returns.

got, err := ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter(data)
if err != nil { t.Fatal(err)
}

And last we compare expectation and result.

if !cmp.Equal(want, got) { t.Error(cmp.Diff(want, got))
}

If everything is correct we can use Run on Go Playground or go test in the command line and the test should pass or return an error.

=== RUN TestParseImageAnalysisResponse
=== PAUSE TestParseImageAnalysisResponse
=== CONT TestParseImageAnalysisResponse
{0.69146144 Lion 682 109 707 186}
--- PASS: TestParseImageAnalysisResponse (0.00s)
PASS

Full listing

package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" "github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp" "testing"
) type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions []PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"`
} type PredictionsAdapter struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` YMin int `json:"y_min"` XMin int `json:"x_min"` YMax int `json:"y_max"` XMax int `json:"x_max"`
} var data = []byte(`{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }`) func ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter(data []byte) (ResponseAdapter, error) { var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter err := json.Unmarshal(data, &responseAdapter) if err != nil { fmt.Println(err) } fmt.Println(responseAdapter.Predictions[0]) return responseAdapter, nil
} func TestParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter(t *testing.T) { t.Parallel() want := ResponseAdapter{ Success: true, Predictions: []PredictionsAdapter{ { Confidence: 0.69146144, Label: "Lion", YMin: 682, XMin: 109, YMax: 707, XMax: 186, }, { Confidence: 0.7, Label: "Penguin", YMin: 782, XMin: 209, YMax: 717, XMax: 196, }, }, } got, err := ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter(data) if err != nil { t.Fatal(err) } if !cmp.Equal(want, got) { t.Error(cmp.Diff(want, got)) } }

Now we have the data available in such a format that we can actually use it. We could even convert it to JSON and pass it to our user client. But sometimes the implementation logic requires that we add or remove some fields and provide the data in a different format. If that is what makes most sense, we should do it to maintain a well designed data workflow, even if it takes some additional work.

In the next section we will define the types Response and Predictions in a way we actually need them and transform them from our Adapter types using custom UnmarshallJSON. First we will add some custom metadata. Adding or changing fields in []PredictionsAdapter however requires looping through each element in the array.

Implementing custom UnmarshallJSON

Let’s create the Test for ParseImageAnalysisResponse that we are going to implement next (ignore any errors). Out want will be a Response that contains some metadata like name and Xid and the array of predictions. We use the data to pass it to ParseImageAnalysisResponse and than see if our expectation was met.

func TestParseImageAnalysisResponse(t *testing.T) { t.Parallel() want := Response{ Name: "Test Image", Xid: "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g", Predictions: []Prediction{ { Confidence: 0.69146144, Label: "Lion", X: 109, Y: 682, Width: 77, Height: 25, Method: "AI", }, { Confidence: 0.7, Label: "Penguin", X: 209, Y: 782, Width: -13, Height: -65, Method: "AI", }, }, } got, err := ParseImageAnalysisResponse(data) if err != nil { t.Fatal(err) } if !cmp.Equal(want, got) { t.Error(cmp.Diff(want, got)) } }

Next we define types that match the JSON API response we want to relay to our client, that could be a web app or a mobile app for Android or iOS.

type Prediction struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` X int `json:"x"` Y int `json:"y"` Width int `json:"width"` Height int `json:"height"` Method string `json:"method"` // AI, Manual, Predicted(?)
}

The Response will contain many of those predictions, since many objects can be detected in an image. But further more it will include meta data like a Name and an Xid. A Xid is a unique identifier that is a related but shorter version of UUID. We might need to change the type later, but for now let’s use string.

type Response struct { Name string `json:"name"` Xid string `json:"xid"` Predictions []Prediction `json:"predictions"`
}

Now we will adapt the ParseImageAnalysisResponse function. It will still use the data of type []byte as an argument, but instead of a ResponseAdapter it will now return our new Response. Therefore we use a variable response of type Response and use this when unmarshalling. We then check for errors and return the response.

func ParseImageAnalysisResponse(data []byte) (Response, error) { var response Response // Use custom UnmarshalJSON method to obtain Response err := json.Unmarshal(data, &response) if err != nil { fmt.Println(err) } return response, nil }

Since there is no easy 1-to-1 mapping from the ResponseAdapter to the Response, we need to do some manual work. Instead of the default UnmarshalJSON which we don’t have to specify, we will now use a customized version of it that explicitly states how we can derive our Response to the client from the data in the ResponseAdapter.
The data we send to ParseImageAnalysisResponse is the one we want to unmarshall. We know that we can unmarshall it into a ResponseAdapter, so thats what we will do first, but not into the pointer of the response object r, but into a temporary variable tmp.

var tmp ResponseAdapter
err := json.Unmarshal(data, &tmp) if err != nil { fmt.Println(err)
}

This variable also has the Predictions in form of a PredictionsAdapter. We now cast them into a new Predictions-Array.

We do this by looping though each element PredictionsAdapter

for _, element := range tmp.Predictions {

and create a temporary corresponding predictions element.
This predictions element also defines the data for any added field or it could also leave out fields from PredictionsAdapter. Then we append the Predictions element to the Predictions Array p, that was defined before the loop. I did variable creation and appending kind of in one step here and closed the loop.

p = append(p, Prediction{ Confidence: element.Confidence, Method: "AI", Label: element.Label, XMin: element.XMin, XMax: element.XMax, YMin: element.YMin, YMax: element.YMax, })
}

Then we set the Response fields. That includes metadata like the name and Xid and setting the predictions array p.

r.Name = "Test Image"
r.Xid = "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g"
r.Predictions = p

And last since UnmarshalJSON requires us to return an error, we do that as well.

Now lets see how this function looks in its full glory.

func (r *Response) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error { var tmp ResponseAdapter err := json.Unmarshal(data, &tmp) if err != nil { fmt.Println(err) } var p []Prediction for _, element := range tmp.Predictions { p = append(p, Prediction{ Confidence: element.Confidence, Method: "AI", Label: element.Label, X: element.XMin, Y: element.YMin, Width: element.Width(), Height: element.Height(), }) } r.Name = "Test Image" r.Xid = "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" r.Predictions = p return err
}

Conclusion

Great. We can now take an image that was annotated with an AI microservice and make this analysis useful for relaying it the user client by enhancing it with more relevant data for a JSON response. On the networking side how to exactly send JSON responses from a HTTP request to a client was explained . I talked about how to send POST requests to the AI micrososervice in a previous post.

Last, let’s look at the full listing, which you can find also on Go Playground. The tests could also be written into a separate file of course.

Thanks for reading, I hope you found this useful. This has been one of the first five posts on my new blog, so I appreciate any comments and suggestions.

package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" "github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp" "testing"
) type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions []PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"`
} type PredictionsAdapter struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` YMin int `json:"y_min"` XMin int `json:"x_min"` YMax int `json:"y_max"` XMax int `json:"x_max"`
} func (p PredictionsAdapter) Width() int { return p.XMax - p.XMin
} func (p PredictionsAdapter) Height() int { return p.YMax - p.YMin
} type Prediction struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` X int `json:"x"` Y int `json:"y"` Width int `json:"width"` Height int `json:"height"` Method string `json:"method"` // AI, Manual, Predicted(?)
} type Response struct { Name string `json:"name"` Xid string `json:"xid"` Predictions []Prediction `json:"predictions"`
} func (r *Response) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error { var tmp ResponseAdapter err := json.Unmarshal(data, &tmp) if err != nil { fmt.Println(err) } var p []Prediction for _, element := range tmp.Predictions { // fmt.Println("element", element, "at index", index) p = append(p, Prediction{ Confidence: element.Confidence, Method: "AI", Label: element.Label, X: element.XMin, Y: element.YMin, Width: element.Width(), Height: element.Height(), }) } r.Name = "Test Image" r.Xid = "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" r.Predictions = p return err
} var data = []byte(`{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }`) func ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter(data []byte) (ResponseAdapter, error) { var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter err := json.Unmarshal(data, &responseAdapter) if err != nil { fmt.Println(err) } fmt.Println(responseAdapter.Predictions[0]) return responseAdapter, nil
} func ParseImageAnalysisResponse(data []byte) (Response, error) { var response Response // Use custom UnmarshalJSON method to obtain Response err := json.Unmarshal(data, &response) if err != nil { fmt.Println(err) } // fmt.Println("Response") // fmt.Println(response) return response, nil } func TestParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter(t *testing.T) { t.Parallel() want := ResponseAdapter{ Success: true, Predictions: []PredictionsAdapter{ { Confidence: 0.69146144, Label: "Lion", YMin: 682, XMin: 109, YMax: 707, XMax: 186, }, { Confidence: 0.7, Label: "Penguin", YMin: 782, XMin: 209, YMax: 717, XMax: 196, }, }, } got, err := ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter(data) if err != nil { t.Fatal(err) } if !cmp.Equal(want, got) { t.Error(cmp.Diff(want, got)) } } func TestParseImageAnalysisResponse(t *testing.T) { t.Parallel() want := Response{ Name: "Test Image", Xid: "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g", Predictions: []Prediction{ { Confidence: 0.69146144, Label: "Lion", X: 109, Y: 682, Width: 77, Height: 25, Method: "AI", }, { Confidence: 0.7, Label: "Penguin", X: 209, Y: 782, Width: -13, Height: -65, Method: "AI", }, }, } got, err := ParseImageAnalysisResponse(data) if err != nil { t.Fatal(err) } if !cmp.Equal(want, got) { t.Error(cmp.Diff(want, got)) } }

Links

https://bitfieldconsulting.com/golang/map-string-interface
https://jhall.io/posts/go-json-tricks-array-as-structs/
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/42377989/unmarshal-json-array-of-arrays-in-go
item.author: royalswish

item.title: MongoDB $weeklyUpdate #81（2022 年 8 月 5 日）：了解内容、播客和视频！ | MongoDB $weeklyUpdate #81 (August 5, 2022): Catch Up on Content, Podcasts, and Videos!
item.description:
欢迎来到 MongoDB $weeklyUpdate！

在这里，您将找到最新的开发人员教程、即将举行的官方 MongoDB 活动，并提前了解由Megan Grant策划的我们最新的 Twitch 直播和播客。

享受！

想要查找开发人员为开发人员创建的最新 MongoDB 教程和文章？ 看看我们的开发中心吧！

在本教程中，我们将了解如何使用 .NET Core 与 GraphQL API 进行交互。

使用我们的 Atlas App Services 应用程序创建一个适合我们业务逻辑的完整推送通知系统。

在本教程中，我们将了解如何将 MongoDB Node.js 驱动程序与 Vercel 函数一起使用，这在开发 Next.js 应用程序时很常见。

在本教程中，我们将了解如何使用 Netlify Functions 和 MongoDB Atlas 构建和部署一个简单的 Web 应用程序。

一旦暂停，mongodb 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，mongodb 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，mongodb 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 mongodb 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

Welcome to MongoDB $weeklyUpdate!

Here, you'll find the latest developer tutorials, upcoming official MongoDB events, and get a heads up on our latest Twitch streams and podcast, curated by Megan Grant.

Enjoy!

—

🎓 Freshest Tutorials on Dev Center

Want to find the latest MongoDB tutorials and articles created for developers, by developers? Look no further than our Dev Center!

Interact with a GraphQL API from a .NET Core Application

@nraboy
In this tutorial, we'll see how to use .NET Core to interact with a GraphQL API.

Push Notifications Using Atlas App Services & iOS Realm SDK

Josman Pérez Expóstio
Use our Atlas App Services application to create a complete push notification system that fits our business logic.

Integrate MongoDB into Vercel Functions for the Serverless Experience

@nraboy
In this tutorial, we're going to see how to use the MongoDB Node.js driver with Vercel functions, something quite common when developing Next.js applications.

Developing a Web Application with Netlify Serverless Functions and MongoDB

@nraboy
In this tutorial, we're going to see how to build and deploy a simple web application using Netlify Functions and MongoDB Atlas.

📅 Official MongoDB Events & Community Events

Once suspended, mongodb will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, mongodb will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by mongodb will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If mongodb is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
